verses.text: The people make Jehoahaz king, but Necho deposes him after three months and sets up his brother as Jehoiakim, exacting tribute. Judah's kings are now puppets of Egypt. The nation that forsook God has lost the freedom He once secured for it.

The people make Jehoahaz king, but Necho deposes him after three months and sets up his brother as Jehoiakim, exacting tribute. Judah's kings are now puppets of Egypt. The nation that forsook God has lost the freedom He once secured for it.

The people make Jehoahaz king, but Necho deposes him after three months and sets up his brother as Jehoiakim, exacting tribute. Judah's kings are now puppets of Egypt. The nation that forsook God has lost the freedom He once secured for it.

Jehoiakim does evil; Nebuchadnezzar binds him and carries some of the temple vessels to Babylon. The slide toward exile quickens. As kings grow more faithless, the holy things are stripped away and the end draws nearer.

Jehoiakim does evil; Nebuchadnezzar binds him and carries some of the temple vessels to Babylon. The slide toward exile quickens. As kings grow more faithless, the holy things are stripped away and the end draws nearer.

Jehoiakim does evil; Nebuchadnezzar binds him and carries some of the temple vessels to Babylon. The slide toward exile quickens. As kings grow more faithless, the holy things are stripped away and the end draws nearer.

Jehoiachin reigns briefly and does evil, then is carried to Babylon with the temple's precious vessels, and Zedekiah is set up in his place. Another king falls, more treasure lost. Each unrepentant reign hastens the judgment long foretold.

Jehoiachin reigns briefly and does evil, then is carried to Babylon with the temple's precious vessels, and Zedekiah is set up in his place. Another king falls, more treasure lost. Each unrepentant reign hastens the judgment long foretold.

Zedekiah does evil, rebels against Babylon, and stiffens his neck against the LORD, while priests and people transgress greatly and pollute the temple. The whole nation, high and low, abandons God. When even the holy place is defiled, the cup of sin is nearly full.

Zedekiah does evil, rebels against Babylon, and stiffens his neck against the LORD, while priests and people transgress greatly and pollute the temple. The whole nation, high and low, abandons God. When even the holy place is defiled, the cup of sin is nearly full.

The LORD sends His messengers, rising up early, because He had compassion on His people; but they mock the prophets and despise His words, 'till there was no remedy.' Mercy pleads to the very last. Judgment falls only when grace, long resisted, is finally refused.

The LORD sends His messengers, rising up early, because He had compassion on His people; but they mock the prophets and despise His words, 'till there was no remedy.' Mercy pleads to the very last. Judgment falls only when grace, long resisted, is finally refused.

The Chaldeans come, slaying without pity, burning the temple, breaking down the walls, and carrying off all the treasures. The glory of Jerusalem is consumed. Sin's long account is settled at last in fire and ruin.

The Chaldeans come, slaying without pity, burning the temple, breaking down the walls, and carrying off all the treasures. The glory of Jerusalem is consumed. Sin's long account is settled at last in fire and ruin.

The Chaldeans come, slaying without pity, burning the temple, breaking down the walls, and carrying off all the treasures. The glory of Jerusalem is consumed. Sin's long account is settled at last in fire and ruin.

The survivors are carried to Babylon as servants, and the land keeps her sabbaths seventy years, fulfilling the word of Jeremiah. Even in judgment God's word stands exact. The rest the people denied the land, He now exacts, and not a year of His promise fails.

The survivors are carried to Babylon as servants, and the land keeps her sabbaths seventy years, fulfilling the word of Jeremiah. Even in judgment God's word stands exact. The rest the people denied the land, He now exacts, and not a year of His promise fails.

In Cyrus's first year the LORD stirs his spirit to proclaim the rebuilding of the temple and to send the exiles home: 'who is there among you of all his people? The LORD his God be with him.' The book ends not in ruin but in hope. The God who judged remembers mercy and opens the door home.

In Cyrus's first year the LORD stirs his spirit to proclaim the rebuilding of the temple and to send the exiles home: 'who is there among you of all his people? The LORD his God be with him.' The book ends not in ruin but in hope. The God who judged remembers mercy and opens the door home.
